files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,12 @@
+# Skill and Process Issues
+
+## 2026-04-09
+
+### Inline extraction prompts are fragile when staged through tool output
+
+- Phase: `extracting-requirements`
+- Trigger: Preparing multiple extraction batches for subagents by reading several batch payloads through `exec_command` and then pasting them into agent prompts.
+- Observed behavior: The local tool output truncated one batch payload (`BATCH-002`) mid-file, which meant the extraction subagent received incomplete canonical spec text even though the batch file on disk was complete.
+- Why this matters: The extraction skill requires passing chunk content inline to subagents rather than having them read files. In Codex, that makes the workflow sensitive to tool-output token limits during prompt staging, and partial payloads are easy to miss until the extracted stories under-cover the source.
+- Workaround used: Discard the affected extraction result, restage each batch individually with enough output budget to include the full payload, and only then dispatch the subagent.
+- Suggested fix: Add a documented Codex-specific staging method for extraction payloads that avoids silent truncation, or provide a helper script that emits one batch payload per call with an integrity marker so the orchestrator can verify completeness before dispatch.
